source_paper_bibtex: @article{Mohammadi_2025, title={Parabolic Regularity of Spectral Functions}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0010}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0010}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Mohammadi, Ashkan and Sarabi, Ebrahim}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={2017–2046} }
context:
Section 5 (discussion immediately after Proposition 5.4), page 25.

To obtain a full characterization of the critical cone of spectral functions, we should know when the matrices \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) in Proposition 5.4 have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition since the critical cone \(K_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\) can be often calculated rather easily. While this remains an open question for now and will be a subject of our future research, we show by an example below the possible role that the condition on \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) is playing in the calculation of the critical cone of spectral functions.
title: Characterize simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions needed for spectral-function critical cones
background_latex:
Let \(\mathbb{S}^n\) be the space of real \(n\times n\) symmetric matrices. For \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), let \(\lambda(X)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denote the vector of eigenvalues sorted in nonincreasing order. Write an eigen-decomposition \(X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top\) with \(U\in O_n\). Let \(\mu_1>\cdots>\mu_r\) be the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), and define index blocks
\[\alpha_m:=\{i\in\{1,\dots,n\}:\lambda_i(X)=\mu_m\},\qquad m=1,\dots,r,\]
so \(\{1,\dots,n\}=\bigcup_{m=1}^r \alpha_m\) is a partition.

A spectral function is a function \(g:\mathbb{S}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) of the form \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), where \(\theta:\mathbb{R}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) is symmetric (permutation-invariant). Assume \(g\) is convex and lower semicontinuous, and let \(Y\in\partial g(X)\) be a (convex-analytic) subgradient. A known characterization (for convex spectral functions) implies there exists an orthogonal matrix \(U\in O_n(X)\cap O_n(Y)\) such that
\[X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top,\qquad Y=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))U^\top,\qquad \lambda(Y)\in\partial\theta(\lambda(X)).\]
For each block \(\alpha_m\), define the corresponding principal submatrices
\[\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}:=\big(\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))\big)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\qquad H_{m}:=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\]
where \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\) is a direction and \(U_{\alpha_m}\) denotes the submatrix of \(U\) with columns indexed by \(\alpha_m\).

Two symmetric matrices \(A,B\in\mathbb{S}^k\) are said to have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition if there exists \(Q\in O_k\) such that \(Q^\top A Q\) and \(Q^\top B Q\) are both diagonal with diagonal entries sorted in nonincreasing order (equivalently, they are simultaneously diagonalizable in a way compatible with the eigenvalue orderings).
problem_definition_latex:
Give necessary and sufficient conditions (checkable from spectral data and/or algebraic relations) ensuring that, for a given convex spectral function \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), a point \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), a subgradient \(Y\in\partial g(X)\), and a direction \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\), the following property holds:

For every block \(m=1,\dots,r\) associated with the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), the pair of matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.

Equivalently, characterize when there exist orthogonal matrices \(Q_m\in O_{|\alpha_m|}\) such that both \(Q_m^\top \Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m} Q_m\) and \(Q_m^\top H_m Q_m\) are diagonal with their diagonal entries arranged in nonincreasing order.
latex:
Let \(\mathbb{S}^n\) be the space of real \(n\times n\) symmetric matrices. For \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), let \(\lambda(X)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denote the vector of eigenvalues sorted in nonincreasing order. Write an eigen-decomposition \(X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top\) with \(U\in O_n\). Let \(\mu_1>\cdots>\mu_r\) be the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), and define index blocks
\[\alpha_m:=\{i\in\{1,\dots,n\}:\lambda_i(X)=\mu_m\},\qquad m=1,\dots,r,\]
so \(\{1,\dots,n\}=\bigcup_{m=1}^r \alpha_m\) is a partition.

A spectral function is a function \(g:\mathbb{S}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) of the form \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), where \(\theta:\mathbb{R}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) is symmetric (permutation-invariant). Assume \(g\) is convex and lower semicontinuous, and let \(Y\in\partial g(X)\) be a (convex-analytic) subgradient. A known characterization (for convex spectral functions) implies there exists an orthogonal matrix \(U\in O_n(X)\cap O_n(Y)\) such that
\[X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top,\qquad Y=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))U^\top,\qquad \lambda(Y)\in\partial\theta(\lambda(X)).\]
For each block \(\alpha_m\), define the corresponding principal submatrices
\[\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}:=\big(\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))\big)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\qquad H_{m}:=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\]
where \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\) is a direction and \(U_{\alpha_m}\) denotes the submatrix of \(U\) with columns indexed by \(\alpha_m\).

Two symmetric matrices \(A,B\in\mathbb{S}^k\) are said to have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition if there exists \(Q\in O_k\) such that \(Q^\top A Q\) and \(Q^\top B Q\) are both diagonal with diagonal entries sorted in nonincreasing order (equivalently, they are simultaneously diagonalizable in a way compatible with the eigenvalue orderings).

Give necessary and sufficient conditions (checkable from spectral data and/or algebraic relations) ensuring that, for a given convex spectral function \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), a point \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), a subgradient \(Y\in\partial g(X)\), and a direction \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\), the following property holds:

For every block \(m=1,\dots,r\) associated with the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), the pair of matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.

Equivalently, characterize when there exist orthogonal matrices \(Q_m\in O_{|\alpha_m|}\) such that both \(Q_m^\top \Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m} Q_m\) and \(Q_m^\top H_m Q_m\) are diagonal with their diagonal entries arranged in nonincreasing order.
open_problem_quote: To obtain a full characterization of the critical cone of spectral functions, we should know when the matrices \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) in Proposition 5.4 have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition since the critical cone \(K_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\) can be often calculated rather easily. While this remains an open question for now and will be a subject of our future research, we show by an example below the possible role that the condition on \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) is playing in the calculation of the critical cone of spectral functions.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The property concerns existence of a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition, which can be tested numerically for random small matrices by checking commutativity/degeneracy structure and order compatibility, so automated searches for patterns or minimal obstructions are feasible. However, turning such experiments into a general characterization may require symbolic reasoning about eigenvalue multiplicities and ordering constraints beyond finite instance search.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can encode candidate algebraic characterizations (e.g., commutator conditions plus tie-handling rules) and score them on randomly generated instances where the decomposition existence can be decided computationally, providing an automatic fitness signal. The search space for correct necessary-and-sufficient conditions is large and the ordering aspect introduces case distinctions, so evolutionary search may help conjecture forms but is unlikely to fully solve the problem without substantial theoretical input.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Mohammadi–Sarabi (2024), the ordered simultaneous diagonalization condition appears as the key remaining structural ingredient in the critical-cone description for convex spectral functions \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\). Specifically, Proposition 5.4 shows that \(H\in K_g(X,Y)\) is equivalent to (i) the reduced-space condition \(\lambda'(X;H)\in K_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\) and (ii) for each multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the pair \((\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m},\,U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m})\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. The paper explicitly notes that giving intrinsic, checkable necessary-and-sufficient conditions for this blockwise ordered simultaneous decomposition is open, and it is precisely this gap that the present problem asks to fill.

Forward-citing work largely treats the ordered simultaneous decomposition requirement as a black-box condition embedded in critical-cone or second-order formulas. The paper “Characterizations of tilt-stable local minimizers of a class of matrix optimization problems” uses the same blockwise ordered simultaneous diagonalization condition to characterize critical directions for polyhedral \(\theta\), and derives concrete block-structure consequences by refining each \(\alpha_m\) according to equal eigenvalues of \(Y\) within the block. “Twice Epi-Differentiability of Spectral Functions and its applications” extends the same critical-cone characterization beyond convexity, again with the ordered simultaneous decomposition as the decisive extra condition. In the singular-value analogue, “Twice epi-differentiability of orthogonally invariant matrix functions and application” provides an iff critical-cone characterization featuring blockwise simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions, typically tied to equality cases in Fan/von Neumann trace inequalities; this strongly suggests that the eigenvalue-only case should admit a similarly checkable characterization, but an explicit standalone criterion for the symmetric-eigenvalue blocks is not provided in the forward-citation summaries.

Overall, the state of the art indicates the problem remains open: existing papers identify where the ordered simultaneous decomposition is needed (critical cones, second subderivatives, tilt stability) and sometimes translate it into block-structural consequences once such a decomposition exists, but none supplies a complete intrinsic characterization (purely in terms of spectral data and algebraic relations of \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\)) guaranteeing the existence of an ordered common eigenbasis on each \(\alpha_m\). Promising directions include exploiting equality characterizations in Fan’s inequality \(\langle A,B\rangle\le \langle \lambda(A),\lambda(B)\rangle\) on each block, commutator-based criteria for simultaneous diagonalizability (augmented with order-compatibility constraints when eigenvalues repeat), and refining the block partitions induced by ties in \(\lambda(Y)\) to reduce the ordered condition to diagonalizability within smaller invariant subspaces.

sources.title: Characterizations of tilt-stable local minimizers of a class of matrix optimization problems
sources.theorem_or_result:
For convex polyhedral spectral functions of the form \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\) on \(\mathbb S^n\) with \(\theta\) symmetric polyhedral, and for \((X,Y)\in\mathrm{gph}\,\partial g\) satisfying a nonemptiness condition \(K(X,Y)\neq\emptyset\), the paper proves (Remark 4.10(ii) citing/using [30, Prop. 5.4]) that a direction \(H\in\mathbb S^n\) lies in the critical cone \(C_g(X,Y)\) if and only if:
- \(\lambda'(X;H)\in C_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\), and
- for each eigenvalue block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the pair \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(P_{\alpha_m}^\top H P_{\alpha_m}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.
In addition, under \(K(X,Y)\neq\emptyset\) it computes a minimal quadratic bundle element
\[q(H)=\Upsilon_{X,Y}(H)+\delta_{\mathrm{aff}\,C_g(X,Y)}(H),\]
where \(\Upsilon_{X,Y}(H)=2\sum_{1\le l<l'\le r}\sum_{i\in\alpha_l,\,j\in\alpha_{l'}}\frac{\lambda_i(Y)-\lambda_j(Y)}{\lambda_i(X)-\lambda_j(X)}(P^\top H P)_{ij}^2\).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for necessary and sufficient, checkable conditions ensuring that for each eigenvalue block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admit a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.

This citing paper does not give a standalone algebraic characterization of simultaneous ordered diagonalizability for arbitrary \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\). However, it directly uses and highlights an equivalence from the cited work [30] (Parabolic Regularity of Spectral Functions): membership \(H\in C_g(X,Y)\) is equivalent to (i) a condition on \(\lambda'(X;H)\) and (ii) exactly the blockwise simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition property in the open problem (Remark 4.10(ii)). It further reformulates (ii) into an explicit block-structure condition (Remark 4.10(ii)') in terms of the partition \(\gamma_s^l\) induced by equal eigenvalues of \(Y\) within each \(\alpha_l\), i.e., \(P_{\alpha_l}^\top H P_{\alpha_l}\) must be block diagonal along the \(\gamma\)-partition and each block can be orthogonally diagonalized with eigenvalues ordered compatibly across blocks.

Thus the paper provides partial progress: it embeds the desired simultaneous ordered decomposition condition into a full critical-cone characterization for polyhedral spectral functions and gives concrete structural consequences, but it does not fully answer the open problem’s request for intrinsic necessary-and-sufficient conditions purely in terms of spectral/algebraic relations for the pair \((\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m},H_m)\) in complete generality (e.g., without reference to critical cones / \(\theta\)-data, and for general convex spectral functions beyond polyhedral \(\theta\)).

sources.title: Twice epi-differentiability of orthogonally invariant matrix functions and application
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper develops second-order variational analysis for convex orthogonally invariant matrix functions \(F=f\circ\sigma\) (singular-value functions). A key result directly tied to the open problem is Proposition 5.2: for \(Y\in\partial(f\circ\sigma)(X)\), a direction \(H\) lies in the critical cone \(K_{f\circ\sigma}(X,Y)\) iff \(\sigma'(X;H)\in K_f(\sigma(X),\sigma(Y))\) and, for each nonzero singular-value block \(\alpha_i\), the pair \(\Sigma(Y)_{\alpha_i\alpha_i}\) and \(\tfrac12(U_{\alpha_i}^\top H V_{\alpha_i}+V_{\alpha_i}^\top H^\top U_{\alpha_i})\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition, while on the zero block \(\beta\) the pair \(\Sigma(Y)_{\beta\hat\beta}\) and \(U_{\hat\beta}^\top H V_{\beta}\) admits a simultaneous ordered singular value decomposition. The paper also gives exact formulas for second subderivatives under parabolic regularity assumptions (Theorem 5.6) and twice epi-differentiability for the nuclear norm (Corollary 3.6).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for necessary and sufficient, checkable conditions ensuring that for each eigenvalue-multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of a symmetric matrix \(X\), the pair \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\) has a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. This paper provides such a condition (via Fan’s inequality equality characterization) but in the singular-value/orthogonally invariant setting: Proposition 5.2 characterizes exactly when the required blockwise simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions occur (they are part of the iff description of the critical cone). Although stated for \(f\circ\sigma\) and blocks of equal singular values, the mechanism is the same as in the symmetric spectral-function case \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\): equality cases in Fan/von Neumann inequalities force simultaneous ordered diagonalizations on multiplicity blocks. Thus it gives a concrete necessary-and-sufficient criterion (commonly checkable as equality in the relevant trace inequality, equivalently existence of a common ordered eigenbasis) for the blockwise property—covering the analogous condition needed in spectral-function critical cones, but not explicitly specialized/proved for the eigenvalue-only (\(\lambda\)) framework in full generality.

sources.title: Twice Epi-Differentiability of Spectral Functions and its applications
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper proves a critical-cone characterization for (possibly nonconvex) spectral functions and uses it to derive a second-order theory. In particular (Proposition 3.2): under mild regularity (e.g. subdifferential regularity of \(g\) or local Lipschitzness of \(\theta\) with \(Y\in\partial \hat g(X)\)), \(H\in C_g(X,Y)\) iff \(\lambda'(X;H)\in C_\theta(\lambda(X),v)\) and, for each eigenvalue block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the pair \(\mathrm{Diag}(y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition (for \(Y=U\mathrm{Diag}(y)U^\top\)).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for necessary and sufficient, checkable conditions ensuring that for each multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admit a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. This paper does not give an intrinsic algebraic/spectral-data-only criterion (e.g. in terms of commutators, eigenvalue multiplicities, or majorization relations) for when such an ordered simultaneous diagonalization exists. However, it elevates exactly this property to a necessary-and-sufficient condition for membership in the spectral-function critical cone \(C_g(X,Y)\) (Proposition 3.2), extending prior convex results. Thus it partially advances the open problem by identifying the ordered simultaneous decomposition requirement as the precise additional condition (beyond \(\lambda'(X;H)\in C_\theta\)) governing critical directions, but it leaves open the requested explicit characterization of when the ordered simultaneous decomposition holds.

sources.title: Smoothness of subgradient mappings and its applications in parametric optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: For reliably \(C^2\)-decomposable functions \(g\) (a class including many spectral/eigenvalue functions), strict proto-differentiability of the subgradient mapping is characterized by a relative-interior condition: for \((\bar u,\bar y)\in\operatorname{gph}\partial g\), \(\partial g\) is strictly proto-differentiable near \((\bar u,\bar y)\) (equivalently, \(g\) is strictly twice epi-differentiable near \((\bar u,\bar y)\)) iff \(\bar y\in\operatorname{ri}\partial g(\bar u)\) (Theorem 5.8). As a concrete spectral-cone instance, for \((X,Y)\in\operatorname{gph}N_{\mathbb S_+^n}\), \(N_{\mathbb S_+^n}\) is strictly proto-differentiable near \((X,Y)\) iff \(Y\in\operatorname{ri}N_{\mathbb S_+^n}(X)\), equivalently \(\operatorname{rank}X+\operatorname{rank}Y=n\) (Corollary 5.10).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for necessary/sufficient conditions ensuring that, on each eigenvalue-multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), the pair \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. This paper does not directly characterize simultaneous ordered diagonalizability of \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\). However, it develops a relative-interior criterion for strict proto-differentiability of subgradient mappings of \(C^2\)-decomposable functions, and explicitly notes that many eigenvalue/spectral functions fall in this class. In the spectral-function setting, strict proto-differentiability/strict twice epi-differentiability is closely tied (in the original ‘Parabolic Regularity of Spectral Functions’ program) to blockwise spectral structure conditions on \(Y\) and directions \(H\). Thus the paper provides a high-level, checkable condition \(Y\in\operatorname{ri}\partial g(X)\) that is sufficient/necessary for strict proto-differentiability, but it does not translate this into the requested blockwise “simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition” condition involving \(H\).

sources.title: Twice epi-differentiablity and parabolic regularity of a class of non-amenable functions
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops second-order variational analysis (parabolic epi-differentiability, twice epi-differentiability, and parabolic regularity) for composite functions of the form \(f(x)=\vartheta(F(x))\) where \(\vartheta\) is piecewise twice differentiable (PWTD) and \(F\) is parabolically semidifferentiable. Under MSQC and parabolic regularity, it proves proper twice epi-differentiability with formula
\[
 d^2 f(x\mid v)(w)=\begin{cases}
 \min_{z}\{d^2 f(x)(w\mid z)-\langle v,z\rangle\} & w\in C_f(x,v),\\
 \infty & \text{otherwise},
 \end{cases}
\]
and provides sufficient conditions (Theorem 5.1) ensuring parabolic regularity via upper/lower estimates of second subderivatives.
sources.connection: The open problem asks for necessary and sufficient conditions guaranteeing, within each eigenvalue-multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of a symmetric matrix \(X\), that the diagonal block \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) (from a subgradient \(Y\in\partial g(X)\)) and the rotated direction block \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admit a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. This paper does not address that simultaneous ordered diagonalization question directly. However, it is closely related in spirit: it studies parabolic regularity and twice epi-differentiability for non-amenable composites, and it explicitly treats a spectral example (type IV: \(F(X)=\lambda_{\max}(X)\), \(\vartheta=\delta_{\mathbb R_-}\)) and cites work on parabolic regularity of spectral functions. The techniques (parabolic semidifferentiability, second-order tangent/critical cone analysis, and conditions ensuring parabolic regularity) are tools that could be applied to spectral functions \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), where the need for simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions arises when analyzing second-order objects on eigenvalue-multiplicity blocks.

sources.title: Variational Analysis in Spectral Decomposition Systems
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops an abstract framework of “spectral decomposition systems” \(\mathfrak S=(X,S,\gamma,(\Lambda_a)_{a\in A})\) and proves transfer formulas for generalized normals/subdifferentials of spectral sets/functions. In particular, for an \(S\)-invariant \(\varphi:X\to[-\infty,+\infty]\) and \(X\in\mathcal H\), it shows
\[\partial^\#(\varphi\circ\gamma)(X)=\{\Lambda_a y: y\in\partial^\#\varphi(\gamma(X)),\ a\in A_X\},\]
for \(\#\in\{F,L\}\) (Theorem 4.1), and gives a Clarke-subdifferential representation (Proposition 4.11) plus a generalized Lidskii-type majorization theorem (Theorem 5.1).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for necessary and sufficient conditions ensuring that, for each repeated-eigenvalue block \(\alpha_m\) of a symmetric matrix \(X\), the pair \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) (a diagonal matrix of subgradient eigenvalues restricted to the block) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admit a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. This paper does not address such blockwise simultaneous ordered diagonalization conditions, nor does it analyze critical cones/parabolic regularity conditions that hinge on ordering-compatible simultaneous diagonalizations within eigenspaces.

However, it is tangentially relevant as it provides general variational-analysis transfer principles for spectral functions/sets (Fréchet/limiting/Clarke subdifferentials, commutation principles) in a unified framework that includes the symmetric-eigenvalue setting. These tools could be used in future work to re-derive first-order objects appearing in critical-cone descriptions, but they do not resolve the specific ordering/simultaneous-decomposition characterization requested.
